summary: This is a brief announcement post directing readers to Cathy O'Neil's Bloomberg View column about regulating technology and algorithms in the United States. The post indicates that America cannot simply follow Europe's regulatory approach (likely referring to GDPR) but suggests Senator Mark Warner has proposed viable alternatives. The substantive analysis appears in the linked Bloomberg article rather than this blog post itself.
key_points: The US needs its own approach to data and algorithm regulation rather than copying Europe
Senator Mark Warner has proposed ideas worth considering for tech regulation
The 'Wild West' framing suggests current US data practices are lawless and need governance
money_quotes: How America Can Stop Being the Wild West of Data
It can't follow Europe's example. But Senator Mark Warner has some good ideas.
key_insight: America needs homegrown solutions for regulating technology and data rather than importing European frameworks wholesale.
